product: New Relic NerdGraph NRQL API (asynchronous queries)

transport.rule: HTTPS POST of {query, variables}; the rendered NRQL travels as a GraphQL variable, never spliced into the GraphQL document

auth.rule: the key travels only in the API-Key header and must never appear in URLs, GraphQL documents, or error messages

submit.async_flag: async: true opts the query into the asynchronous longer-running workflow
submit.timeout_rule: the timeout variable is sent only when the caller provides one; async is always true
submit.fast_path: when nrql.results is non-null on the first response the query finished within the timeout and no polling happens

query_progress_units.retryAfter: seconds to wait before the next poll
query_progress_units.retryDeadline: absolute epoch seconds after which the query progress can no longer be polled
query_progress_units.resultExpiration: absolute epoch seconds after which completed results are dropped

poll.account_rule: the account argument must match the account argument from the original query
poll.verbatim_rule: the poll request can be repeated verbatim until results or an error are returned - consecutive poll request bodies must be byte-identical
poll.wait_source: sleep queryProgress.retryAfter seconds between polls, through the injected sleeper only

cancel.deadline_rule: when the injected clock reaches retryDeadline before completion, the client sends one nrqlCancelQuery mutation and raises QueryDeadlineError

metadata_fields.rule: metadata returned by NerdGraph is preserved on the QueryResult exactly as received

nrql_safety.allowed_types: str, bool, int, float, and lists/tuples of those
nrql_safety.string_rule: string parameters render single-quoted; strings containing a single quote, a backslash, or control characters are rejected with UnsafeParameterError before any request
nrql_safety.bool_rule: True/False render as the NRQL literals true/false
nrql_safety.number_rule: ints render as decimal integers; floats must be finite (NaN/inf rejected)
nrql_safety.list_rule: lists render as a parenthesized comma-separated tuple, e.g. ('a', 'b')
nrql_safety.placeholder_rule: a placeholder with no matching parameter, or a parameter with no matching placeholder, is an UnsafeParameterError

fixtures.template: SELECT count(*) FROM Transaction WHERE appName = {app} AND httpResponseCode IN {codes} FACET name SINCE {hours} hours ago
fixtures.rendered: SELECT count(*) FROM Transaction WHERE appName = 'checkout-web' AND httpResponseCode IN ('500', '502') FACET name SINCE 6 hours ago

graphql_error.message: NRQL Syntax Error: Error at line 1 position 8, unexpected 'FRUM'
